title: lagerstroemia lanceolata
intro: Lagerstroemia lanceolata, also known as the narrowleaf crape myrtle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Southeast Asia, known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its health benefits, including antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. Lagerstroemia lanceolata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts for medicinal use.
